notes: Gemini CLI /clear (alt /new) mints a fresh session id and materializes a NEW session-<ts>-<id8>.jsonl under the same chats/ dir; the old file stops being appended. Models the proc-PID → session-1 → /clear → session-2 lifecycle. Birth phase anchored to start UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal it. old_session_ended asserts the #169 same-PID cleanup emits transcript_removed for the FIRST session within 1s of the new session appearing (one launcher PID, session id rotates). v2 phases pinned to v2_session_birth via same_session_as so a stale working transition on session-1 can't satisfy them. The new session binds the launcher PID via cwd harvested from the new file's <session_context>. BACKFLOW (recorded 2026-06-12): VERIFIED — /clear mints a NEW session-<ts>-<id8>.jsonl (new session_id) bound to the SAME launcher PID, and the #169 same-PID cleanup DOES emit transcript_removed for the OLD session. TIMING CORRECTION: the cleanup does NOT fire within 2s of the new session appearing — it lands ~12s later, when the new session re-binds the launcher PID after its own activity (HandlePIDAssigned). old_session_ended is therefore anchored to v2_session_birth with a wide max_delay_ms (20s) rather than 2s. Observed model is gemini-3.5-flash (API-key tier), not gemini-3-flash-preview — corrected.

text: Session row appears in ready (pre-session proc-<PID> or the session-id-keyed row) within 2s
Launcher PID bound within 3s (cwd harvested from <session_context>)
First user prompt drives ready → working on session-1
First turn completes back to ready (same session-1)
Spec: 'New session appears in ready with a NEW session_id' — /clear mints a fresh session id and a new session-<ts>-<id8>.jsonl never seen before

Spec: 'Old session disappears' — the #169 same-PID cleanup emits transcript_removed for the FIRST session id within 2s of the new session appearing (one launcher PID, id rotates). Bounds the visible overlap.
Second user prompt drives the NEW session ready → working (must NOT match a stale working transition on session-1)
Second turn completes back to ready on the new session
